Recommendations:

We recommended  for  the  City  Accounting  Office  to  locate  the  missing
voucher, verify/check the validity of the remaining  reconciling  items  and
make the necessary accounting journal entries.

We also recommended for the CAccO to comply with the timely preparation  and
submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the  complete
supporting  documents  such  as   the   original   Bank   Statements,   Bank
Debit/Credit Memos and other relevant documents.

Recommendations:

We recommended for the CHRD to submit the required documents  to  the  CAccO
to facilitate the recording/adjustments  of  the  correct  balances  of  the
Other Receivables account. We also recommended  furnishing  a  copy  of  all
ongoing housing contracts to the COA Office.

Recommendations:

We recommended for the CHO to furnish a copy of the PTR and/or a  report  to
the  CAccO  and  the  CGSO  for  the  medical  supplies/properties  directly
received by them from the DOH XI and/or other government agencies  for  that
matter which  should  include  the  utilization/issuances  thereof  and  the
balances as at year-end.

We also recommended for the CAccO to  make  the  necessary  entries  in  the
books to take up the balance of the supplies received from DOH XI, if  there
are any, and to  expense  the  issuances  of  the  Agricultural  and  Marine
Supplies for Distribution.

We recommended further for the implementing offices  concerned  to  adopt  a
systematic procedure in the preparation and submission of a report  relative
to the receipts and issuances/distribution of  supplies/properties  directly
received by them including donated goods and products to the CAccO  and  the
CGSO to facilitate the timely recording in the books  of  accounts  and  for
accountability purposes.

Recommendations:

We recommended for the following courses of actions:

     1. For the CAccO and the CGSO in coordination with the City Assessor’s
        Office (CAssO) and the City Legal Office to  trace  the  supporting
        documents and facilitate the titling of the land in the name of the
        City;

     2. For the CAccO to reconcile their records with the CAssO to obtain a
        reliable description and cost of some of the land listed herein and
        effect the necessary accounting/adjusting journal entries; and

     3. Refrain from financing any development or improvements to the  land
        with legal issues/encumbrances and questionable ownership to  avoid
        wastage of government funds.

Recommendations:

We recommended for the  City  Accounting  Office  to  effect  the  necessary
accounting entries to recognize/correct the  depreciation  expense  and  the
related accumulated depreciation accounts.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council, through the LDRRM Office, to include the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

      We recommended for the City Mayor to:


    • Direct the City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s  Office  to
      exhaust all possible means and efforts  to  locate  the  missing  paid
      vouchers which will support the unaccounted  withdrawals/disbursements
      and to make  the  necessary  accounting  journal  entries,  otherwise,
      impose   the   appropriate    penalty    against    the    responsible
      officials/employees.;


    • Direct the City Accounting Office and its personnel assigned to  exert
      efforts  to  account  all  unrecorded  debit/credit  memos  and  other
      unrecorded  reconciling  items,  retrieve  the   previous   accounting
      records, coordinate with the bank  for  possible  re-printing  of  the
      memos and other means to identify, and eventually  account/record  the
      total amount of the reconciling items; and


    • Direct the City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s  Office  to
      conduct   regular  reconciliation  of  their  records  and  make   the
      necessary adjustments in both records if any.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that Management ensure strict adherence  to  the  laws,
rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation  of  cash
advances and to:


      •  Enforce  the  immediate  liquidation  and/or  settlement  of   all
        outstanding cash advances. Cause the withholding of the salaries of
        AOs who  could  not  submit  liquidation  documents  without  valid
        reasons and impose the administrative  sanctions  provided  in  CSC
        Memorandum Circular No. 23 s. 2019;


      • Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to the  same
        officials/employees who have previous  unliquidated  cash  advances
        regardless of the purpose; and


      • Request write-off  of  long  outstanding  cash  advances  that  are
        considered dormant in consonance with the guidelines and procedures
        provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendations:


      We recommended for the City Mayor to:

      • Direct the City Treasurer’s Office to furnish the  City  Accounting
        Office with a certified list of taxpayers at the beginning  of  the
        year to serve as the basis in  setting  up  of  real  property  tax
        receivables and the balances  as  at  year-end  for  reconciliation
        purposes; and


      • Direct the City Accounting Office to keep and  maintain  subsidiary
        ledgers of individual taxpayers.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that Management ensure the City’s compliance  with  the
LRAM and the following courses of actions:

    • Direct the City Accounting Office to observe the proper  recording  of
      local road networks,  particularly  the  requirement  on  providing  a
      complete description of the asset and to submit the following:


     a) Latest records of the Registries  of  Public  Infrastructures,  not
        just for Roads but all Infrastructure Assets;
     b) Updated Schedule of Road Networks, showing  the  individual  assets
        complete with description and its components;


    • Direct the City Accounting Office to prepare the necessary  accounting
      journal entries to reclassify the water system costing P145,395.00  to
      its proper account and to adjust the accumulated depreciation  of  the
      Road Networks taking into consideration  the  correct  computation  of
      depreciation without the provision of residual/scrap value;


    • Direct the CEO and the CGSO (a) to conduct a  physical  count  of  all
      local roads, (b) to prepare an updated Local Road Inventory  and  Road
      Map and (c) to keep complete records of Local  Road  Network  Property
      Card; and


    • Direct the City Accounting Office, the CEO and the CGSO to  coordinate
      and reconcile their records.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that City Accounting Office:

    • Coordinate with the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) to  determine  if
      there are still taxes owed by the City to the BIR. If it is determined
      that the City no longer has taxes  due  for  remittance  to  the  BIR,
      prepare the necessary  adjustments  either  to  refund  these  to  the
      respective taxpayers or revert these to the surplus account, whichever
      is appropriate. Otherwise, remit the balance of taxes due  immediately
      to the BIR; and

    • Prepare and  maintain  subsidiary  ledgers  for  each  type  of  taxes
      withheld and regularly monitor the remittances thereof to  avoid  late
      remittances which may incur penalties and surcharges.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Management to strictly  comply  with  the  existing
laws on the proper withholding and timely remittance of PhilHealth and  Pag-
IBIG premiums/loans and to:

      • Require the City  Accounting  Office  and  the  CHRMDO  to  conduct
        periodic/regular  reconciliation/analysis   of   the   inter-agency
        payable  accounts,  refund  the   employees   for   over   deducted
        premiums/loans, if applicable; and


      • Adjust the related accounts/records to reflect the correct balances
        of the individual accounts of the employees.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the  City  Accounting  Office  to  reconcile  the  PRDP
accounting records with the related disbursements and  liquidations  and  to
effect the necessary adjusting journal entries  to  arrive  at  the  correct
balance of the Due to NGAs account.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that the City Accounting Office:


    • Conduct detailed review of the unutilized/unmoving balances  amounting
      to P7,440,842.46, coordinate with the source agency to facilitate  the
      return/remittance of the unutilized amount to  the  National  Treasury
      and/or transfer the unutilized amount to the General  Fund,  whichever
      is appropriate; and


    • Maintain an updated subsidiary ledgers  for  liabilities  account  and
      regularly update/submit the Certificate of Status for fund transfers.

Recommendations:

       We  recommended  the  City  Accounting  Office  to  be  cautious   in
monitoring  the  loans  payable  to  avoid  the  incurrence  of  unnecessary
penalties and loss/wastage of funds.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the CHRMDO:

      • Require the Job Orders to  submit  valid  IDs  and  TIN  number  in
        addition to the Personal Data Sheet being submitted every  contract
        renewals. Have these ID numbers be indicated  in  the  Contract  of
        Service. The contracts  should  also  be  attested  by  the  City’s
        Administering Officer or Authorized Personnel; and


      • Require all personnel, staff and JOs  to  strictly  adhere  to  the
        CSC’s policy on government working hours particularly on the manner
        of recording the actual time ins/outs. Install Bundy/Time Clock  or
        Biometrics Machine to monitor and record  the  employees'  and  Job
        Orders’ actual arrival and departure  from  office/station.  Ensure
        that the necessary access  controls  are  in  place  to  prevent  a
        fraudulent access/alterations of these records.

      We also recommended that the City Accounting Office  personnel  review
the claims for salaries and wages and ensure that these  are  complete  with
the required supporting documents.

Recommendations:


      We recommended for the Management and the LSB to stop the practice  of
charging administrative and other unallowable expenses against the  SEF  and
to strictly adhere to the rules  and  regulations  on  the  use  of  Special
Education Fund.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that Management fully institutionalize  the  LDRRMO  by
filling  up  the  mandatory  positions  to  assure  the  implementation   of
activities and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

      We also recommended, subject to availability of  funds,  appropriating
funds for the created plantilla positions in the LDRRMO and/or  augment  the
existing personnel through the options enumerated in Section 5.5 of  NDRRMC-
DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that the City Accounting Office  and  the  City  Budget
Office to coordinate with each other in the  determination  of  the  correct
balance of unexpended CY 2019 LDRRMF (MOOE and QRF) and  to  facilitate  the
transfer of the amount to the Special Trust Fund account maintained for  the
purpose.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Management strictly comply with  the  existing
rules and regulations on  the  utilization  of  LDRRMF  and  to  submit  the
revised LDRRMFIP which should include PPAs pertaining to the  unexpended  CY
2018 LDRRMF.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the City Engineering Office and/or  the  officials
concerned to submit the required documents, such  as  (a)  pictures  of  the
work/accomplishments,  (b)  Inspection  Report,  and  (c)   Certificate   of
Completion, to support and validate the project.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

    • Enjoin the Officials and Employees concerned to  strictly  follow  the
      rules and regulations on the proper use of  government  vehicles/heavy
      equipment, install effective control over expenditures for  fuel,  oil
      and lubricants;


    • Direct the City Accounting Office that the assigned  personnel  ensure
      the required supporting documents for fuel expenses are  complete  and
      proper, such as the duly accomplished withdrawal slip, charge invoices
      and approved, duly accomplished and  serially-numbered  Driver’s  Trip
      Tickets;


    • Direct the personnel in-charged  at  the  motor  pool  to  maintain  a
      logbook to control and monitor the movement of all  the  vehicles  and
      heavy equipment of the City. The logbook should contain the  necessary
      information  such  as  the  plate  number,  the  description  of   the
      vehicle/heavy equipment in the absence of the plate  number,  and  the
      time of arrival and departure; and


    • Require the personnel concerned to submit the Charge/Sales Invoice for
      the procured fuel and oil totaling  P9,365,000.00  pertaining  to  the
      Land  Development/Survey/Titling  project  and  all  other   pertinent
      documents/reports to substantiate the expenditures.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

      • Require the Officials/Personnel concerned to  submit  the  required
        supporting documents for the projects listed herein, such as:


        a. Resolutions and/or evaluations conducted to  undertake  projects
           by administration
        b. List of actual accomplishments  which  should  be  specific  and
           quantifiable
        c. Program of Work and/or Detailed Estimates or Detailed  Breakdown
           of ABC
        d. Statement of Work Accomplished
        e. Inspection Report
        f. Pictures before, during and after the work
        g. Certificate of Completion


      •  Enjoin  the  Implementing  Office/s  concerned  in   relation   to
        implementing projects by administration to strictly comply with the
        documentation required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Revised IRR
        of RA 9184; and


      • Direct the City Accounting Office to review  and  ensure  that  the
        documentation for expenditures relating to  the  implementation  of
        projects by administration is complete and proper before processing
        such claims.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the City Assessor’s Office  revisit  the  Schedule
of Fair Market Values (SMV) of real properties in the locality of the  City,
and make the necessary revision. Also, undertake  the  general  revision  of
property   assessments   and   submit   the   same   to   the    appropriate
Offices/Authorities for possible enactment and approval  of  an  appropriate
ordinance.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Management:


      • Direct the  officials  and  employees  of  Mati  Public  Market  to
        strictly comply, implement and enforce the provisions of the Market
        Code of the City of Mati;


      • Require the existing stallholders to present/submit their  Contract
        of Lease, otherwise, they have to enter into a  Contract  of  Lease
        with the City subject to the City’s existing rules and regulations;


      • Enforce the collections of delinquent rental fees plus penalty  and
        surcharges, otherwise, impose the penal provisions  of  the  Market
        Code.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Management direct the Officers/Office  concern
to stop the practice of improper collection of  Bidding  Documents  Fee  for
procurements which are not conducted through public bidding or  not  any  of
the alternative methods of procurements that utilize process and  procedures
in  competitive  bidding.  Also,  require  the  BAC  to  submit  a   written
explanation as to why the existing practice came about  and  to  return  the
fees  that  were  improperly  collected  to  the  supplier  and/or   service
providers.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Management observe the  timely  submission  of
financial reports and budget accountability reports.


      We also recommended that the City Accounting Office and its  personnel
strictly observe the proper accounting  procedures  such  as  (a)  providing
complete descriptions in the JEV, (b) submitting the JEV together  with  the
liquidation  reports  and  the  RCDs  and  (c)  submitting  the  JEVs   with
supporting documents for transactions recorded in the General Journal.

Recommendations:


      We recommended for the Local Chief  Executive  to  ensure  the  City’s
faithful compliance with the localization  of  the  Magna  Carta  of  Women,
particularly to:


      • Ensure that  the  GAD  Focal  Point  System  is  in  place,  fully-
        functional and its members  have  the  capacity  and  influence  to
        promote gender mainstreaming;


      •  Ensure that the local officials and employees are  responsible  in
        promoting and ensuring that gender and development is  mainstreamed
        in   local   policy-making,   planning,   programming,   budgeting,
        implementation, monitoring and evaluation; and


      • Direct  the  GFPS  to  conduct  the  gender  analysis  through  the
        administration of the HGDG, so that a proper identification of  the
        gender-responsive programs  and  projects  of  the  City  could  be
        facilitated, and eventually come up with a gender-responsive GPB.

Recommendation:

       We  recommended  that  the  Management  strictly  complied  with  the
requirements set forth on the suspended transactions and enforcement of  the
immediate settlement of the disallowed transactions in full to  recover  the
amounts of unauthorized claims.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
That the Management must establish adequate control measures in their disbursement procedure, particularly in the handling of paid vouchers and the supporting documents, to ensure that all disbursements for the period will be properly accounted for and/or recorded in the books of accounts.
For the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to coordinate their efforts in locating for the missing DVs and SDs.
For the City Accountant to look into the inconsistencies noted in the reconciling items of the bank reconciliation statements, provide explanation as to its incurrence and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the DVs and SDs that have been found.
For the City Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements in a timely manner as prescribed in the existing COA rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct all accountable officers with outstanding petty cash as of year-end the immediate liquidation thereof and to refrain the granting of petty cash to accountable officers who failed to settle or liquidate their outstanding petty cash.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to appoint a committee to assess the collectability of the subject receivable accounts that would provide a reasonable amount for the Allowance of Impairment to be recognized in the books of the City.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action for the Local Chief Executive, to wit:
To direct the GSO to properly monitor the utilization of the inventories including the timely preparation and submission of the SSMI to the Accounting Office.
To direct the Accounting Office to immediately record the appropriate accounting entries in the books to recognize the issued inventories based on the SSMI received from the GSO.
To direct the GSO and Accounting Office to follow the Perpetual Inventory method in accounting the purchase of supplies and materials.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management the following courses of action:
That the City Accountant’s Office and the City General Services Office must establish an effective or practical system that would ensure the expeditious and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the City’s Property, Plant and Equipment;
For the City Accountant to verify the items in the Construction in Progress account and make the necessary adjustments in the books to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate PPE account; and
That the Local Chief Executive should augment the needed personnel and equipment in the General Services Office and Accounting Office that would facilitate in the reconciliation and efficient maintenance of records of the PPE accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the BACO, to meticulously plan the PPAs to be undertaken for the year under the BIDA program and provide specific information of these PPAs in the Annual Budget and in the AIP.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Due to NGAs account under the Trust Fund books for each Funding Agency and for each individual project, and coordinate or reconcile its records with the implementing department concerned.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary Ledger per project for the SLRF deposited in the Cash-in-Bank account of the Regular Trust Fund in lieu of a separate bank account to be maintained for the said purpose.

Recommendations:
We recommended the management the following courses of action:
To strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the utilization of its LDRRMF.
To meticulously plan the PPAs to be incorporated in their LDRRMFIP maximizing the use of the current year’s LDRRMF and prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Accountant:
To verify and identify the transfers and utilization made from prior years’ unexpended DRRMF as well as from other sources to facilitate the maintenance of the subsidiary ledgers thereof.
To reconcile the balance of the Cash-in-Bank LBP Account No. 1022-1099-24 with the balance of the unexpended LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
That the City Mayor should prioritize the construction of the Leachate Treatment facility in the sanitary landfill so as to make it operative and facilitate the discontinued use of the controlled dumps as a procedure in solid waste management.
For the management to strictly adhere to the provisions of the R.A. No. 9003.

Recommendation:
We recommended the officials of the City School Board to observe frugality in the utilization of the SEF and discontinue appropriating funds for expenses which are not among those prioritized to be charged against the SEF to preclude suspensions and/or disallowance.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the offices concerned that supply requirements should be meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that no government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.
Furthermore, we recommended the management to refrain from purchasing supplies and materials through reimbursement and/or drawing of cash advance, and strictly adhere with the pertinent guidelines in procurement as provided in the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking documents and effect corrective measures to address the deficiencies noted.
Finally, we recommended the Agency to strictly adhere with the bidding procedures as provided in the Revised IRR R.A. No. 9184 to ensure regularity of the procurement process.

Recommendations:

We also recommended the agency officials concerned to include the reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the Budget Office in the flow of transactions in the disbursement of funds with emphasis on control procedures.

We recommended the Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office to exhaust their efforts in locating the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents pertaining to the unrecorded withdrawals reflecting in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for LBP Account No. 1022-1000-80 and LBP Account No. 1022-1055-62.

We also recommended the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books for the stale checks included in the Outstanding Checks on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation:
Demand the settlement of the outstanding cash advances for travels immediately without prejudice of enforcing the consequence of failure the same as provided for under Item 3.3.2b. of COA Circular No. 96-004 implementing Section 16 of Executive Order No. 248 quoted as follows:

3.3 MONITORING OF CASH ADVANCES FOR TRAVEL

       3.3.2b. Delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated, if the official or employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period.

  The Office of the City Accountant and other officers/personnel processing claims should strictly  observe the directive under Item 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-004 including those limitations set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and other related laws, rules and regulations in order to enforce the consequence for failure to liquidate said cash advances quoted as follows:

3.3
MONITORING OF CASH ADVANCES FOR TRAVEL

   3.3.1 The Accountant shall retain a copy of the travel advance voucher and the itinerary of travel (Appendix A). He shall keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee and shall monitor the liquidation of the said cash advances.

   3.3.2 In addition, the Accountant shall:

   Send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 or 60 days period specified under Section 16 of EO 248, a written reminder under signature of the head of the agency or his duly authorized representative enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his travel cash advance. This is to preclude complaints arising from suspension of salaries due to non-liquidation of travel advances.

  Require the City Accountant to conduct an extensive, exhaustive verification and evaluation of the unliquidated cash advances and other collectible to determine their probable collectivity and to come up with appropriate recommendations thereon including the possible writing off of the established doubtful accounts pursuant to Section 36 of P.D. No. 1445.
